message:
feat(demo_showcase): enhance files page and documentation

- files.rs: Improved file browser functionality
- welcome.md: Updated welcome documentation
- Add e2e test artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1wtl","title":"Files: preview pane (viewport)","description":"Add a preview pane for the selected file using `bubbles::viewport`.

Why:
- Makes the Files page feel like a real tool (inspect configs/logs/docs quickly).
- Exercises viewport scrolling + resize behavior.

Preview behavior:
- For plain text:
  - show a bounded preview (first N lines/bytes) with a "truncated" indicator
  - choose a clear wrapping strategy (wrap-by-default is fine; optionally support horizontal scroll)
- Show lightweight metadata (optional but nice):
  - file size, last modified, detected type (text/markdown/binary)
- Resize handling:
  - preview reflows correctly on window resize

Acceptance:
- Large files do not lock up the UI; preview work is bounded.
- Scrolling the preview is smooth and does not interfere with file picker navigation.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.317694097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:01:53.925382291Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.337282014Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:14.307425706Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1x3q","title":"Jobs: start/cancel/retry actions via Cmd/AsyncCmd","description":"Make jobs interactive.

Implement actions:
- Start a new job (creates a job + progress updates)
- Cancel job
- Retry failed job

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so UI code stays thin.
- bubbletea `Cmd` for sync simulation.
- bubbletea `AsyncCmd` when async feature enabled.

Acceptance:
- Actions produce toasts and update other pages (dashboard/logs).
- Action layer is unit tested independently of the UI.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.940826293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:04.939573406Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.955289813Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:30:38.522320902Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:46.346531972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:49.013962965Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xvj","title":"demo_showcase core: Notes scratchpad modal (bubbles::TextArea)","description":"Exercise `bubbles::TextArea` in a way that feels like a real product feature.

Feature idea:
- A modal "Notes" scratchpad to write an incident note / deploy note.
- Supports multi-line editing, copy, clear, save.
- Saving the note emits a structured log entry and a toast.

Why this belongs in core:
- Provides a compelling demo of text editing beyond single-line inputs.
- Gives users a practical pattern they can copy into real apps.

Acceptance:
- Accessible via a global key (defined in UX keymap).
- Uses `bubbles::TextArea` (not huh) so we prove both stacks.
- Saving integrates with the log stream + notifications.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.295951524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:46.342882675Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","textarea","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:46.342854462Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:43.822250299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.315341950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:45.081658950Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:42.571125789Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:41.284606640Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ynf","title":"Wizard: design the multi-step workflow","description":"Design the wizard flow so it demonstrates real value.

 {"id":"bd-1ywx","title":"glamour: Validate language aliases on registration","description":"# Task: Validate Language Aliases on Registration

## Problem Statement
The language_alias() builder method accepts any strings without validation:

```rust
// Lines 623-630
pub fn language_alias(mut self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    self.syntax_config
        .language_aliases
        .insert(alias.into(), language.into());
    self
}
```

No checks for:
1. Whether target language is actually supported
2. Whether alias forms a cycle (A -> B -> A)
3. Whether alias shadows a built-in language name

## Impact Analysis
- **Silent failures**: Alias to non-existent language just doesn't highlight
- **Confusing behavior**: Cycles could cause infinite loops or unexpected results
- **Debugging difficulty**: No error message explains why highlighting isn't working

## Solution Design

### Validation on Registration
```rust
pub fn language_alias(
    mut self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError> {
    let alias = alias.into();
    let language = language.into();
    
    // Check target language exists
    if !self.syntax_set_contains(&language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(language));
    }
    
    // Check for cycles
    if self.would_create_cycle(&alias, &language) {
        return Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { alias, language });
    }
    
    self.syntax_config.language_aliases.insert(alias, language);
    Ok(self)
}

fn would_create_cycle(&self, alias: &str, target: &str) -> bool {
    let mut current = target;
    let mut visited = HashSet::new();
    visited.insert(alias);
    
    while let Some(next) = self.syntax_config.language_aliases.get(current) {
        if !visited.insert(next.as_str()) {
            return true;  // Cycle detected
        }
        current = next;
    }
    false
}
```

### Backward Compatibility Option
Keep existing method but add validated version:
```rust
// Existing (for compatibility)
pub fn language_alias(self, alias: impl Into<String>, language: impl Into<String>) -> Self

// New validated version
pub fn try_language_alias(
    self,
    alias: impl Into<String>,
    language: impl Into<String>,
) -> Result<Self, GlamourError>
```

## Implementation Steps
1. Add validation logic to language_alias()
2. Add GlamourError variants for invalid language and cycles
3. Consider deprecating unchecked version
4. Update documentation with valid language list
5. Add tests for validation

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn alias_to_invalid_language_fails() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "nonexistent");
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::InvalidLanguage(_))));
}

#[test]
fn alias_cycle_detected() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("a", "b")?
        .try_language_alias("b", "c")?
        .try_language_alias("c", "a");  // Creates cycle
    assert!(matches!(result, Err(GlamourError::AliasCycle { .. })));
}

#[test]
fn valid_alias_succeeds() {
    let result = GlamourRenderer::new()
        .try_language_alias("rs", "rust");
    assert!(result.is_ok());
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Invalid target language detected with clear error
- [ ] Alias cycles detected and rejected
- [ ] Backward-compatible API (old method still works)
- [ ] Documentation lists valid language names
- [ ] Tests cover validation scenarios","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.883578694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902063497Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["configuration","glamour","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ywx","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:21:13.902020466Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-201t","title":"demo_showcase page: Files (filepicker + preview)","description":"A file browser screen with a preview pane — like a real “open a config/log file” tool.

Why:
- File navigation + preview is a common real-world TUI use case.
- It’s a perfect integration demo:
  - `bubbles::filepicker` for navigation
  - `bubbles::viewport` for preview scrolling
  - `glamour` for markdown preview
  - `lipgloss` for two-pane layout + styling
  - mouse scroll and focus interactions

Must showcase:
- File picker:
  - directory navigation, back/forward, open/select
  - filtering by allowed types (e.g., md, toml, log)
  - show hidden toggle (if supported) or a demo-level filter
- Preview pane:
  - plain text preview with wrapping toggle
  - markdown preview via glamour for `.md`
  - stable scrolling independent of the picker
- Robust UX:
  - clear error handling (permissions, unreadable files, too-large files)
  - toast/status feedback for actions and errors
  - mouse wheel scroll works naturally for the focused pane

Data requirements:
- Use a deterministic fixtures root in demo/test modes so E2E can reliably navigate known files.
- Include a few curated fixture files (markdown, toml, log, and an “error” case) as part of the plan.

Acceptance:
- Feels like a real tool (fast, predictable, and pleasant).
- Preview is readable at multiple terminal sizes.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - selection + navigation state transitions
  - preview selection logic (markdown vs text)
  - error mapping (permission -> friendly message)
- E2E:
  - file picker navigate + preview scenario (verifies both text and markdown preview, captures artifacts).

Notes:
- Keep the UX crisp: this page should be *immediately* useful on its own.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:21.306244015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:42:42.981587564Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-201t","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:21.327764835Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-33fe","title":"demo_showcase data: structured log stream + formatting","description":"Create the underlying structured log stream used by the demo (domain simulation + user actions).

Why:
- The Logs page should not be "just strings". We want structured fields so filtering/search is fast and correct.
- It proves the integration story: domain layer emits events, UI renders + filters them.

Deliverables:
- A `LogEntry` type (or similar) with fields like:
  - timestamp / monotonic tick
  - level
  - service/component
  - message
  - optional correlation ids (job id, deployment id)
- A log buffer/stream:
  - append-only collection with a bounded retention policy (optional)
  - API to push new entries from simulation/actions

Integration:
- The Logs viewer uses this stream and a formatter (`Logs: styled log formatting using charmed_log`) to render.
- Filtering operates on structured fields, not re-parsed rendered text.

Acceptance:
- Large log volumes remain responsive.
- Entries support correlation (wizard → job → logs) and filtering by level/service.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:53.990998123Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336843390Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:32:29.336748333Z","close_reason":"Implemented structured log stream with filtering. Enhanced LogEntry with tick, job_id, deployment_id correlation. Created LogStream with filtering by level/target/job/deployment/text/tick-range, count_by_level(), and bounded retention. Added 16 new tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_log","data","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-278q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:54.007146912Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-33fe","depends_on_id":"bd-2hwq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:48.231233898Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-33mv","title":"demo_showcase UX: define product concept + information architecture","description":"Pick a single cohesive "product" story and define the information architecture.

Recommendation (unless a better idea emerges): **Charmed Control Center** — an ops-style console for a fictional platform (services, deployments, jobs, logs, docs, settings).

Define:
- Primary navigation items (pages) and what each proves about the stack.
- Layout primitives (app chrome): header (status), sidebar (nav), main content, footer (hints).
- Core user journeys:
  - Navigate dashboards, drill into a service/job, view logs, open docs, run a wizard, browse files.

Acceptance:
- Clear page list with 1-2 sentences each describing: goal, components used, and what makes it "real".","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.263653172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678896124Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:15:32.678828398Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive product concept doc at docs/demo_showcase/PRODUCT_CONCEPT.md","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-33mv","depends_on_id":"bd-3kcq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:04.280798177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3449","title":"E2E scenario: wizard -> job -> logs correlation","description":"Scenario: complete the wizard to start a deployment/job, then verify it shows up elsewhere.

Steps:
- Open Wizard
- Fill inputs (including paste for one field)

- Observe job created in Jobs page
- Observe correlated log entries in Logs page

Assertions:

- Logs include fields referencing the created job/deployment.

-{"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

Integration:
- Must plug into the structured artifact logger (`E2E: structured test logging + artifact capture`).

Acceptance:
- Scenarios are readable scripts: "press j", "open docs", "search", "quit".
- Runner failures explain *where* and *why* (step id + last frame + config snapshot).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.886754241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:41.221423312Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-13np","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:37.168687580Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-299e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:03.434318654Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:05.951709495Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:04.674114434Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-pfqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:32.910218872Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-35d2","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:33:07.191517307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-35d2","title":"E2E: headless scripted runner (custom IO + recorded inputs)","description":"Implement an E2E runner that can drive the app without a real TTY.

Core approach:
- Run the app in custom I/O mode (bubbletea `with_input`/`with_output`) so it does not touch the real terminal.
- Feed deterministic input into the program:
  - Prefer a semantic step DSL (key presses, text input, resize, mouse click/scroll).
  - Optionally support raw byte streams to exercise the InputParser explicitly.
- Capture rendered frames (strings) so tests can assert on UI output.
- Terminate deterministically (send quit key / QuitMsg; enforce a max-step guard).

Time determinism:
- Avoid wall-clock sleeps.
- Advance simulation/animations by injecting tick messages and/or running a fixed "frame pump" loop.
- Ensure scenarios can run with animations disabled to reduce flakiness.

Assertions:
- Provide helpers for:
  - `assert_contains("Jobs")` / `assert_not_contains("\x1b[")` (no-color)
  - page header checks
  - optional direct AppState assertions (if the runner has access to the final model)

 {"id":"bd-36vz","title":"Fix unicode width calculation and panic risks in bubbles/glamour","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:30.726729015Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:41.647015306Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:52:41.646959230Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-39bb","title":"Interactions: window resize handling + responsive reflow","description":"Make the whole app responsive.

Implementation:
- App consumes `WindowSizeMsg` and recomputes layout.
- Propagate size to pages/components (viewport/table/list widths/heights).
- Docs page re-renders markdown on width changes.

Acceptance:

 {"id":"bd-39hl","title":"Dashboard: uptime stopwatch + incident countdown widget","description":"Exercise bubbles time components in a realistic, high-polish dashboard context.

Why:
- `bubbles::stopwatch` and `bubbles::timer` are core "real app" primitives (uptime, ETAs, countdowns).
- Time widgets also stress the update loop (tick scheduling, formatting, focus/reduce-motion behavior).

Widgets to implement:
- Uptime (stopwatch):
  - displayed as `DD:HH:MM:SS` (or similar) with compact formatting
  - clearly indicates running/paused state (pause if unfocused is optional)
- Incident SLA / next maintenance window (timer countdown):
  - counts down to an upcoming event
  - visual emphasis as it nears zero (color + subtle animation)

Integration:
- Drive updates via bubbletea tick/every (no manual sleeps).
- Respect global toggles:
  - reduce-motion / `--no-animations` (no flashy pulsing)
  - focus/blur (optionally throttle when unfocused)

Acceptance:
- Widgets look polished and update smoothly.
- No excessive CPU usage (tick frequency is reasonable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.377240967Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:24.071286192Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","stopwatch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-dkff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:24.396412480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-l26a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:26.892799063Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-39hl","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:18:28.950716964Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 Your unified platform for infrastructure observability and operations.

 ## Getting Started
 
 Navigate using keyboard shortcuts or the sidebar:
 
-| Key | Action |
-|-----|--------|
-| `1-7` | Switch pages |
-| `[` | Toggle sidebar |
-| `j/k` | Navigate lists |
-| `Enter` | Select/confirm |
-| `Esc` | Back/unfocus |
-| `?` | Help overlay |
-| `q` | Quit |

+|-----|--------|---------|

+| `j/k` | Navigate lists | Lists/Tables |

+| `Esc` | Back/unfocus | All pages |
+| `?` | Help overlay | Global |
+| `t` | Cycle theme | Global |
+| `q` | Quit | Global |
+
+### Quick Actions
+
+Use these shortcuts for common operations:
+
+- **Filter**: Press `/` to open the filter bar
+- **Sort**: Press `s` to cycle sort columns, `S` for reverse
+- **Refresh**: Press `r` to refresh data
+- **Export**: Press `e` for text, `E` for HTML export
 
 ## Dashboard
 
@@ -23,6 +36,53 @@ The dashboard provides at-a-glance health metrics:
 - **Live Metrics**: Request rate, latency, error rate
 - **Service Status**: Health of all registered services
 - **Recent Activity**: Latest deployments and jobs
+- **Alerts**: Active warnings and incidents
+
+### Metric Cards
+
+Each metric displays:
+
+```
+┌─────────────────────┐
+│ Requests/s          │
+│ ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━  │
+│ 12,847    (+3.2%)   │
+└─────────────────────┘
+```
+
+## Services
+
+Monitor your microservices infrastructure:
+
+| Service | Status | Uptime | Last Deploy |
+|---------|--------|--------|-------------|
+| api-gateway | ● Healthy | 7d 23h | 2h ago |
+| auth-handler | ● Healthy | 14d 5h | 1d ago |
+| billing-worker | ◐ Degraded | 3d 12h | 4h ago |
+| cache-proxy | ● Healthy | 30d 0h | 5d ago |
+
+> **Tip**: Use the status filter (`1-4`) to show only services with specific health states.
+
+### Service Configuration
+
+Services are configured via TOML:
+
+```toml
+[service]
+name = "api-gateway"
+port = 8080
+workers = 4
+
+[health_check]
+interval_seconds = 30
+timeout_seconds = 5
+path = "/health/ready"
+
+[metrics]
+enabled = true
+port = 9090
+path = "/metrics"
+```
 
 ## Jobs
 
@@ -32,29 +92,170 @@ Track and manage background tasks:
 database-backup-001    [==========] 100%  done
 log-rotation-042       [=====>    ]  55%  running
 cache-warmup-013       [          ]   0%  queued
+security-scan-007      [===       ]  30%  running
 ```
 
+### Job Lifecycle
+
+Jobs progress through these states:
+
+1. **Queued** → Waiting for resources
+2. **Running** → Actively executing
+3. **Completed** → Finished successfully
+4. **Failed** → Error occurred (retryable)
+5. **Cancelled** → User-initiated stop
+
 Use `/` to filter, `Enter` to view details.
 
+### Creating Jobs
+
+Jobs can be triggered via the API:
+
+```rust
+use demo_showcase::data::Job;
+
+let job = Job::new("database-backup")
+    .with_schedule("0 0 * * *")  // Daily at midnight
+    .with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(3600))
+    .with_retry_policy(RetryPolicy::exponential(3));
+
+scheduler.submit(job).await?;
+```
+
 ## Logs
 
 Real-time log streaming with color-coded levels:
 
-- **INFO**: Normal operation (blue)
-- **WARN**: Attention needed (yellow)
-- **ERROR**: Requires action (red)
-- **DEBUG**: Verbose output (gray)
+| Level | Color | Description |
+|-------|-------|-------------|
+| `TRACE` | Dim gray | Verbose debugging |
+| `DEBUG` | Gray | Debug information |
+| `INFO` | Blue | Normal operation |
+| `WARN` | Yellow | Attention needed |
+| `ERROR` | Red | Requires action |
+
+### Sample Output
+
+```log
+2024-01-15T10:23:45.123Z INFO  api-gateway: Request received path=/api/users method=GET
+2024-01-15T10:23:45.156Z DEBUG auth-handler: Token validated user_id=42
+2024-01-15T10:23:45.201Z INFO  api-gateway: Response sent status=200 latency_ms=78
+2024-01-15T10:23:46.001Z WARN  billing-worker: High queue depth depth=1523 threshold=1000
+2024-01-15T10:23:47.892Z ERROR cache-proxy: Connection timeout host=redis-01 timeout_ms=5000
+```
+
+### Log Filtering
+
+Filter logs with these keyboard shortcuts:
+
+- `1` Toggle ERROR level
+- `2` Toggle WARN level
+- `3` Toggle INFO level
+- `4` Toggle DEBUG level
+- `5` Toggle TRACE level
+- `/` Text search
+- `c` Clear all filters
 
 Press `f` to toggle follow mode.
 
 ## Wizard
 
 Step-by-step workflows for common operations:
 
-1. Configure deployment parameters
-2. Review and validate
-3. Execute with progress tracking
+1. **Configure** → Set deployment parameters
+2. **Validate** → Review configuration
+3. **Deploy** → Execute with progress tracking
+4. **Verify** → Confirm successful deployment
+
+> **Warning**: Production deployments require approval from a team lead.
+
+### Deployment Configuration
+
+```yaml
+deployment:
+  name: api-gateway
+  environment: production
+  replicas: 4
+
+strategy:
+  type: rolling
+  max_surge: 25%
+  max_unavailable: 1
+
+health_check:
+  initial_delay: 10s
+  period: 5s
+  failure_threshold: 3
+```
+
+## Keyboard Reference
+
+### Global Shortcuts
+
+| Shortcut | Action |
+|----------|--------|
+| `Ctrl+C` | Quit immediately |
+| `?` | Toggle help overlay |
+| `[` | Toggle sidebar |
+| `t` | Cycle theme (Dark → Light → Dracula) |
+
+### Navigation
+
+| Shortcut | Action |
+|----------|--------|
+| `j` / `↓` | Move down |
+| `k` / `↑` | Move up |
+| `g` | Go to top |
+| `G` | Go to bottom |
+| `Ctrl+d` | Page down |
+| `Ctrl+u` | Page up |
+
+### Page Shortcuts
+
+| Key | Page |
+|-----|------|
+| `1` | Dashboard |
+| `2` | Services |
+| `3` | Jobs |
+| `4` | Logs |
+| `5` | Docs |
+| `6` | Wizard |
+| `7` | Settings |
+
+---
+
+## Technical Details
+
+### Framework Stack
+
+Charmed Control Center is built with:
+
+- **bubbletea** - Elm-architecture TUI framework
+- **lipgloss** - Terminal styling and layout
+- **bubbles** - Reusable UI components
+- **glamour** - Markdown rendering
+- **harmonica** - Spring animations
+
+### Data Flow
+
+```
+┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐     ┌──────────┐
+│  Input   │ ──▶ │  Update  │ ──▶ │   View   │
+│  Events  │     │  Model   │     │  Render  │
+└──────────┘     └──────────┘     └──────────┘
+      ▲                                 │
+      └─────────────────────────────────┘
+```
+
+### Performance
+
+The application targets:
+
+- **60 FPS** render loop
+- **< 16ms** per frame budget
+- **< 100MB** memory footprint
+- **Zero allocations** in hot paths
 
 ---
 
-*Built with charmed_rust TUI framework*
